revision.description:
Version number.
The first version of the secret is numbered 1, and all subsequent revisions augment by 1.

data.description: The base64-encoded secret payload of the version.

data_crc32.description:
The CRC32 checksum of the data as a base-10 integer.
This field is only available if a CRC32 was supplied during the creation of the version.

type.description:
Type of the secret.
See the `Secret.Type` enum for a description of values.

values.certificate: List of concatenated PEM blocks. They can contain certificates, private keys or any other PEM block types.
values.key_value: Flat JSON that allows you to set as many first level keys and scalar types as values (string, numeric, boolean) as you need.
values.basic_credentials: Flat JSON that allows you to set a username and a password.
values.database_credentials: Flat JSON that allows you to set an engine, username, password, host, database name, and port.
values.ssh_key: Flat JSON that allows you to set an SSH key.
